preamble: The shift from the near-open front vowel [æ] (ash) to the open central/back vowel [a] or [ɑ] is a famous historical process, most notably known as the TRAP-BATH split in English. It involves the retraction and often lengthening of the vowel in specific phonetic environments.

examples.note: Retraction and lengthening before voiceless fricatives.
Similar split, though often utilizing a more central [aː] rather than a deep back [ɑː].

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English. https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/accents-of-english/A9495FCF7CC13D1D11FADB3DE2F335F6
